text: Recipient
Draft
To attach
Regarding
Inquiry
To confirm
Concise
Formal
To acknowledge
Prompt
The report is ready; therefore, I have attached it here.
The control was tested. However, one point is still open.
In addition, we have updated the exposure limits.
I am writing regarding your inquiry about the figures.
Sergio, I saw the email from Claire at the partner bank. Are you going to reply today?
Yes. I have a first draft, but I want it to sound formal. She asked me to confirm the September exposure and to attach the limit table.
Good. Start with the topic: I am writing regarding your inquiry. Then confirm the figure, and use a connector for the second point.
Right. So: I can confirm the total exposure for September. In addition, I have attached the updated limit table. Is that clear enough?
Very clear and concise. Just acknowledge her deadline, say a prompt reply, since the committee meets on Friday.
Perfect. I will close with I look forward to hearing from you. Then I will send the draft to you before it goes out.
I am writing regarding your inquiry about the September exposure.
I can confirm the figure, and I have attached the updated limit table.
In addition, please find the stress-test summary attached.
Thank you for your patience; the committee meets on Friday.
I look forward to hearing from you. Kind regards, Sergio.

Hi Sergio, it is Marina. A quick note before you send that reply to the partner bank. I have read your draft, and it looks formal and concise -- well done. Two small things: please attach the updated limit table, because Claire asked for it, and confirm the September figure in the very first line. In addition, remember to acknowledge her deadline; the committee meets on Friday, so a prompt reply really matters. One more point -- copy me on the email before it goes out, and I will do a final check. Thanks, talk soon.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. About the email to the partner bank -- I have just sent you the September figures, so you can confirm the exposure with confidence. I have also attached the limit table to my message; please forward it to Claire. One thing to flag: my first draft was too casual, so I made it more formal -- feel free to use my wording. Could you acknowledge Claire's inquiry today? A prompt reply will keep the committee on track for Friday. Thanks, and copy me when you reply.

First, read the inquiry from the partner bank carefully. Then open your reply with the topic. Next, confirm the main figure and attach the file they asked for. After that, add your second point with a connector such as in addition. Finally, acknowledge the deadline and close with a formal sign-off.
